summary: No-ship: the slice still has runtime-critical dangling `artifact-store` dependencies, the documents fitting can still fail setup outright, existing non-default storage roots are no longer migrated/read, and improver-nightly still produces dead `garrison://artifacts/...` links.

findings.title: Default and shipped compositions still depend on the removed `artifact-store` fitting path
findings.recommendation: Remove `../../fittings/seed/artifact-store` from the shipped composition manifests and refresh the corresponding lockfiles before shipping. The same stale dependency is still present in `compositions/dogfood-orch/apm.yml`, `compositions/router-v4/apm.yml`, and existing `apm.lock.yaml` files, so `apm install` will otherwise fail as soon as those compositions are used.

findings.title: `documents` setup still hard-requires the deleted artifact-store CLI
findings.recommendation: Point setup at the vendored `scripts/artifacts.py` beside `documents.py`, or stop shelling out entirely and create the namespace directly. As written, installing `documents` without the removed `artifact-store` fitting exits 1 during setup.

findings.title: Document store no longer honors legacy `artifact-store.storage_root`, so existing compositions can silently read/write the wrong directory
findings.recommendation: Add a compatibility fallback that first checks the `documents` fitting config and then the legacy `artifact-store` selection config, or migrate persisted composition selections in-place. Right now only a `documents` selection is consulted, but shipped compositions still store `storage_root` under the removed `artifact-store` selection.

findings.title: Improver-nightly still publishes `garrison://artifacts/<id>` outputs after the artifact-store surface was removed
findings.recommendation: Switch the proposal output contract to a surviving surface, or keep a compatible reader route for these URIs. With artifact-store views/routes gone, improver-nightly runs still succeed but hand back links the UI no longer resolves.
